The legislators celebrated the milestone of the state university's sesquicentennial. No specific legislation or policy action was taken.

title: My Legislation
raw_text:
My Legislation
As your State Representative, I am always working to improve our Commonwealth's laws and policies. Part of this process includes authoring legislation.
The process begins by sharing what is known as a "Co-Sponsorship Memo" with my colleagues to outline what my legislation intends to do.  This allows other House members who agree with my proposal to sign onto the bill regardless of party.
Once the bill is formally drafted, the House Chief Clerk's office assigns it a number and refers it to one of the House's 27 Committees for further review.
You can learn more about the legislative process by clicking
here
.

Towns to operate Storm Water Management Systems

ai_summary.summary: A Pennsylvania state representative has introduced or co-sponsored 12 numbered bills and 2 resolutions in the current legislative session, all of which have been referred to various House committees but none of which have advanced beyond that stage. The bills cover a range of topics including stormwater management authority for local governments, school van driver licensing, volunteer liability protections, rural transit study, first responder vehicle lighting, energy policy, healthcare integration, farm equipment warranty protections, syringe services programs, and aggravated assault penalties. Several additional bills and resolutions are listed as pending formal introduction.

title: Pittman, Struzzi Celebrate IUP’s 150th Anniversary in Harrisburg
May 20 Officially Designated Jimmy Stewart Day in Pennsylvania, Says Struzzi
Continue Reading
raw_text:
May 20 Officially Designated Jimmy Stewart Day in Pennsylvania, Says Struzzi
May 5, 2026
HARRISBURG – State Rep. Jim Struzzi (R-Indiana) announced today
House Resolution 420
, which designates May 20, 2026, as Jimmy Stewart Day in Pennsylvania, was approved by the House of Representatives.
“Jimmy Stewart represents the best of America,” Struzzi said.  “While his career in the film industry included mainstays in American movie culture, his willingness to put acting on hold to serve in World War II speaks to his character.”
James “Jimmy” Maitland Stewart was a film star of national fame, having starred in over 80 big-screen productions over a 55-year acting career. His most notable role, for which he earned an Oscar nomination, was his portrayal of George Bailey in “It’s A Wonderful Life,” arguably the best holiday movie of all time.
Born on May 20, 1908, and raised in Indiana County, Pa., Jimmy Stewart was the son of Alexander and Elizabeth Stewart, who owned and operated the J.M. Stewart and Company Hardware Store. Jimmy Stewart was an alumnus of Princeton University and, later in life, was presented with Princeton’s highest alumni honor, the Woodrow Wilson Award for outstanding public service.
In 1941, Stewart enlisted in the United States Army Air Corps and was later sent to Europe as commander of a bomber squadron. He attained the rank of brigadier general and was honored with several military service recognition awards, including the Air Medal, the Air Force Distinguished Service Medal and the Distinguished Flying Cross. In 1985, he was awarded the highest civilian honor, the Presidential Medal of Freedom.
“The Jimmy Stewart Museum in Indiana County is a community landmark with national interest attracting over 6,000 visitors last year alone,” Struzzi added.  “This year, the museum will be hosting a special first look at the upcoming moving, ‘Jimmy,’ directed by Aaron Burns with Executive Producer Kelly Stewart Harcourt on May 20. The museum will also be debuting a new rotating exhibit, ‘The Making of Jimmy.’ Marking May 20 as Jimmy Stewart Day adds an additional spotlight on our local hero.”
Representative Jim Struzzi
62nd District
Pennsylvania House of Representatives
Media Contact:  Charlie O’Neill
717.787.1711
coneill@pahousegop.com
repstruzzi.com / Facebook.com/RepStruzzi
Share

ai_summary.summary: The Pennsylvania House of Representatives approved House Resolution 420, designating May 20, 2026, as Jimmy Stewart Day in the state. The resolution was sponsored by Rep. Jim Struzzi, who represents Indiana County, where Stewart was born and where a museum dedicated to him is located. The designation coincides with a special event at the Jimmy Stewart Museum previewing an upcoming film about Stewart's life.

title: Struzzi Votes ‘Yes’ on Bill to Level the Field for High School Sports Playoffs
Continue Reading
raw_text:
Struzzi Votes ‘Yes’ on Bill to Level the Field for High School Sports Playoffs
April 29, 2026
HARRISBURG –State Rep. Jim Struzzi (R-Indiana) shared today to he voted in favor of
HB 41
, legislation which would permit the Pennsylvania Interscholastic Athletic Association (PIAA) to establish separate playoff categories for schools based on whether their student-athlete population is limited to a specific set of geographic boundaries.
“The existing PIAA playoff system is unfair to most Indiana County schools,” Struzzi said. “My vote for HB 41 will permit the PIAA to begin the process of establishing a separate playoff system for schools who can recruit star players without geographic limits. Schools who can recruit across district, county, and state lines should not be playing for the same title against our local public schools whose players are limited to a defined geographical area.”
Struzzi noted this legislation was supported by several local school districts including Purchase Line and Marion Center.
“During the 2025-26 session, I have worked on several reforms in the PIAA system,” Struzzi added.  “Spectators at playoff games can once again pay for tickets with cash, which the PIAA had previously banned. I also led the charge to implement the provisions of my legislation,
HB 1462
, in last year’s budget, which permits hometown broadcasters to broadcast, stream, and provide play-by-play of high school games throughout the playoffs.”
Representative Jim Struzzi
62nd District
Pennsylvania House of Representatives
Media Contact: Charlie O’Neill
717.787.1711
coneill@pahousegop.com
repstruzzi.com / Facebook.com/RepStruzzi
Share

ai_summary.summary: State Rep. Jim Struzzi voted in favor of HB 41, a bill that would allow the PIAA to create separate playoff brackets for schools that can recruit student-athletes from outside defined geographic boundaries versus schools limited to a specific local area. The release also notes two other PIAA-related changes Struzzi claims credit for: restoring cash payment for playoff tickets and securing budget provisions allowing local broadcasters to stream playoff games.

title: Struzzi Lauds the Extension of Key-Con Coal-fired Electric Plants Authorization
Continue Reading
raw_text:
Struzzi Lauds the Extension of Key-Con Coal-fired Electric Plants Authorization
April 22, 2026
INDIANA –State Representative Jim Struzzi issued the following statement regarding President Trump’s announcement that the Key-Con coal-fired electric plants will remain open:
This is a HUGE deal for Indiana County and Pennsylvania. Affordability starts with cheap, abundant, and reliable energy. Losing the Key-Con coal-fired electric plants would have removed all of those elements from Pennsylvania’s electric grid. This is why I have supported the extension of the Key-Con plants by offering formal comments to the Pennsylvania Department of Environmental Protection, advocating for these plants as a legislative leader in the State Capitol, and championing the elimination of the RGGI carbon tax - which would have meant the end of these electric generation plants - as part of the current state budget.  It is also important to acknowledge the major strides and investments the Key-Con plants are making toward reducing their emissions and demonstrating how clean coal can be an energy leader.  Thank you to President Trump and Senator McCormick for preserving these economic engines of Western Pennsylvania, and thank you to Governor Shapiro for understanding that coal is not just part of Pennsylvania’s past; it is also essential to our future.
Representative Jim Struzzi
62nd District
Pennsylvania House of Representatives
Media Contact: Charlie O’Neill
717.787.1711
coneill@pahousegop.com
repstruzzi.com / Facebook.com/RepStruzzi
Share

ai_summary.summary: State Representative Jim Struzzi issued a statement praising President Trump's announcement that Key-Con coal-fired power plants in Indiana County, Pennsylvania will remain open. Struzzi described actions he says he took to support the plants, including submitting comments to the DEP, lobbying at the state capitol, and opposing Pennsylvania's participation in the RGGI carbon pricing program. The release is a statement of support for a federal executive decision, not an announcement of direct legislative action.

title: Struzzi Announces Mobile Office Hours to Assist with Property Tax and Rent Rebate Forms, General State Assistance
Pittman & Struzzi Announce $236,555 in State Funding for Local Fire and EMS
Struzzi Supports Budget Package to Protect Taxpayers and Help Communities
raw_text:
Struzzi Supports Budget Package to Protect Taxpayers and Help Communities
July 12, 2026
HARRISBURG – State Rep. Jim Struzzi (R-Indiana), Chairman of the House Republican Appropriations Committee, voted today to finalize the Fiscal Year 2026-27 Budget, which allocates $50.85 billion for state government operations for the fiscal year that runs through June 30, 2027.
“I am proud to support this year’s state budget package, which meets the needs of our Commonwealth without overspending,” Struzzi said.  “The budget represents a 3.7% increase over the budget last year, well below the 5.7% increase the governor asked for and within the range of the Taxpayer Protection Act.”
Struzzi noted that the extended Fiscal Year 2025-26 impasse and subsequent policy changes influenced the agreement reached today.
“House Republicans secured significant policy victories last year, including ending the governor’s carbon tax proposal, known as the Regional Greenhouse Gas Initiative, reducing regulatory barriers, and implementing anti-fraud measures in Human Services Programs,” Struzzi shared. “These actions led to revenue growth exceeding $1 billion, strengthening our financial position.”
“I am pleased to announce this budget invests substantially in rural Pennsylvania, including $775 million for rural roads and bridges,” Struzzi said. “It also increases career and technical education funding by $10 million, eliminating the trade school waiting list. School districts will receive historic funding increases, with an additional $903 million for PreK-12 education, raising the total to $18.94 billion for FY 2026-27.  School districts within the 62nd House District, Homer-Center, Indiana Area, Penns Manor Area, River Valley, and United are allocated a combined $58.4 million.”
Struzzi emphasized that affordability remains a significant concern for retired Indiana County residents, which is why this budget includes a Cost-of-Living Adjustment (COLA) for Pre-Act 9 retirees, professional firefighters, and police officers receiving pensions.
“Living on a fixed income is challenging, especially for public servants who retired before 2001,” Struzzi added. “This budget provides a COLA for Pre-Act 9 retirees, including many teachers, police officers, and professional firefighters. You served our community; now it is our turn to support you.”
As Chairman of the House Republican Appropriations Committee, Struzzi guided his caucus through the budget process, from the governor’s address to hearings, negotiations, and final approval.
“I am happy to report during our budget hearings it came to our attention the Attorney General’s Office was not receiving the financial support they requested,” Struzzi said.  “This final budget increases their funding by over $20 million, including a $15 million line-item for law enforcement support.”
“Every budget presents challenges, and this year was no exception,” Struzzi concluded. “The Fiscal Year 2026-27 budget achieves a balance by prioritizing fiscal responsibility while meeting the needs of Pennsylvania’s citizens.”
Representative Jim Struzzi
62nd District
Pennsylvania House of Representatives
Share

ai_summary.summary: State Rep. Jim Struzzi voted to approve Pennsylvania's $50.85 billion Fiscal Year 2026-27 state budget, a 3.7% increase over the prior year. The budget includes $775 million for rural roads and bridges, $903 million in additional PreK-12 education funding, a cost-of-living adjustment for certain public pension recipients who retired before 2001, and over $20 million in increased funding for the Attorney General's Office.
